docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
This documentation provides a step-by-step guide for setting up Visual Studio Code as an integrated development environment for collaborative projects. Tailored specifically for macOS users, it covers how to access and manage shared repositories on GitHub to collaborate with other developers. It also explains how to configure built-in extensions to facilitate program writing and enhance productivity.

Visual Studio Code is an integrated development environment (IDE) designed by Microsoft for developers to write, edit and debug code. It supports a wide range of programming languages, including JavaScript, Java, Python, and more.

docs.title: Intended Users
docs.text:
This documentation is targeted specifically towards the following users:

  • Beginner developers without software development experience
  • Intermediate developers with software development experience but have never used Visual Studio Code

docs.title: Prerequisite Knowledge
docs.text:
The documentation assumes the following:

  • Working knowledge of Git and GitHub

Please note that, this documentation only provides instructions for setting up Visual Studio Code as an Integrated Development Environment for your projects. It does not cover any knowledge related to programming languages.

docs.title: Software Requirements
docs.text: Before you begin, please ensure that you already have the following software installed and account set-up: - Visual Studio Code v1.98 or later - Git v2.39 or later - a GitHub account

docs.title: Procedure Overview
docs.text: By reading the main section, you will learn to: - Link a GitHub account to Visual Studio Code - Configure a GitHub Repository - Install, activate, and deactivate a built-in extension - Create a HTML page

docs.title: Typographical Conventions
docs.text:
1. Code snippets that are written in the VSC code editor will be shown like this:
for (let i = 1; i <= 10; i++) {
console.log(i);
}
  1. Command line terminal instructions will be formatted like the following:
user-MacBook-Pro desktop % pwd
  1. File names will be formatted like the following:
index.html

docs.title: Notes and Warning Messages
docs.text:
In this documentation, key message blocks will be highlighted to emphasize important information.

  • Warning: Highlights actions that require careful attention

  • Tip: Offers helpful suggestions or best practices

  • Important: Emphasizes key instructions

  • Example: Displays an example

  • Success: Confirms a successful action
